===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. President Donald Trump recently spoke at the inaugural meeting of his Board of Peace. According to the reports, what was the original intended purpose of this board when it was first announced?
(A) To help end the war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction
(B) To negotiate a new nuclear agreement with the Islamic Republic of Iran
(C) To replace the United Nations as the primary body for international conflict resolution
(D) To coordinate the surge of military forces and aircraft carriers to the Middle East
(E) To manage the diplomatic relations between the US, Israel, and Saudi Arabia
explanation: The article states that when Trump first announced the Board of Peace, "it was thought to be aimed at helping end the two-year war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction."
model_output:
The Board of Peace was established by President Donald Trump in January 2026, with its original intended purpose being to oversee the administration, reconstruction, and economic recovery of the Gaza Strip. This initiative was authorized by United Nations Security Council Resolution 2803, which welcomed the establishment of the Board to support these efforts. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaza_Strip_under_Resolution_2803?utm_source=openai))

At its inaugural meeting on February 19, 2026, the Board focused on pledges for Gaza reconstruction and the deployment of an international stabilization force to ensure security and facilitate rebuilding efforts. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/22e587df67e27cd1e1d96e446cb88378?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the original intended purpose of the Board of Peace was to help end the war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. The US Commission of Fine Arts has approved the design for a new White House ballroom. What is the current planned capacity for this new facility?
(A) 500 people
(B) 1350 people
(C) 2000 people
(D) 150 people
(E) 1200 people
explanation: The article mentions that "The ballroom was initially expected to have capacity of 500 people but it has since been expanded to 1350."
model_output:
The White House State Ballroom, approved by the U.S. Commission of Fine Arts on February 19, 2026, is designed to accommodate approximately 1,000 people. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/f2a15d0b1c9c95f24816fe60b6b1ee5f?utm_source=openai)) This capacity is significantly larger than the East Room's current capacity of about 200 people. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2025/07/31/trump-ballroom-east-wing-overhaul/?utm_source=openai))

The ballroom's design has evolved over time. Initially, it was announced to have a capacity of 650 people. However, in September 2025, President Trump indicated that the ballroom would be expanded to accommodate 900 people. ([the-independent.com](https://www.the-independent.com/news/world/americas/us-politics/trump-ballroom-white-house-campus-capacity-b2826871.html?utm_source=openai)) The most recent information indicates a capacity of approximately 1,000 people. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/f2a15d0b1c9c95f24816fe60b6b1ee5f?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided:

- (A) 500 people
- (B) 1350 people
- (C) 2000 people
- (D) 150 people
- (E) 1200 people

The closest match to the current planned capacity is 1,000 people. Since none of the options exactly match this number, the most appropriate choice is (E) 1200 people, as it is the closest to the actual capacity.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. Official figures show the US trade deficit hit a new high last year. What was the specific deficit amount for goods and services combined?
(A) $1.2 trillion
(B) $3.4 trillion
(C) $202.1 billion
(D) $890 billion
(E) $901.5 billion
explanation: The article notes: "Overall, the deficit in goods and services - which takes into account things like travel and digital services - was $901.5bn last year."
model_output:
In 2025, the United States recorded a trade deficit of $901.5 billion, slightly down from $904 billion in 2024. This figure represents the combined deficit for goods and services. The goods trade deficit reached a record $1.24 trillion, while the services trade surplus increased to $339 billion. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/9eb6bd10ff635d63e46ee99d34ce1d05?utm_source=openai))

Given the options provided, the closest match to the reported deficit is:

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

The film "Porto Rico" is a historical drama directed by René "Residente" Pérez Joglar, featuring Bad Bunny in his first lead acting role. The movie is inspired by the life of José Maldonado Román, a late 19th and early 20th-century revolutionary known as Águila Blanca (White Eagle), who fought against Spanish colonizers in Puerto Rico. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/music/2026/feb/18/bad-bunny-film-role-porto-rico-residente?utm_source=openai))

===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. Etsy has announced the sale of the second-hand fashion app Depop to eBay. For what amount is the app being sold?
(A) $1.6 billion
(B) $217 million
(C) $11.1 billion
(D) $1.2 billion
(E) $890 million
explanation: The article states: "Depop... is being sold to eBay for $1.2bn (£890m)."
model_output:
Etsy has announced the sale of its second-hand fashion app, Depop, to eBay for approximately $1.2 billion in cash. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/b8787b5326cb3a010f4d9e3468ee3171?utm_source=openai)) This strategic move aims to strengthen eBay’s presence among Gen Z consumers, a demographic that heavily uses Depop for affordable, unique, and sustainable fashion. As of the end of 2025, Depop had 7 million active buyers—nearly 90% under age 34—and over 3 million active sellers. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/feb/19/ebay-buys-depop-from-etsy?utm_source=openai))

The sale price of $1.2 billion is lower than the $1.6 billion Etsy paid for Depop in 2021, indicating a loss on the investment. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/feb/19/ebay-buys-depop-from-etsy?utm_source=openai)) Etsy plans to utilize the proceeds for general corporate purposes, continued share repurchases, and investment in its core marketplace. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/b8787b5326cb3a010f4d9e3468ee3171?utm_source=openai))

The deal has been unanimously approved by both companies’ boards and is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/b8787b5326cb3a010f4d9e3468ee3171?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. Billionaire Les Wexner recently gave a deposition regarding his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. Which group of lawmakers attended this session?
(A) Only Democratic lawmakers
(B) Only Republican lawmakers
(C) A bipartisan group of both Democrats and Republicans
(D) Only members of the Senate Oversight Committee
(E) Only lawmakers from Wexner's home state of Ohio
explanation: The article states: "Only Democratic lawmakers attended the deposition. No Republican lawmakers travelled for it, though some of their staff members did attend."
model_output:
Billionaire Les Wexner recently testified before the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee regarding his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. The deposition took place at Wexner's residence in New Albany, Ohio, on February 18, 2026. Democratic members of the committee attended the session, including Ranking Member Robert Garcia, Representatives Stephen Lynch, Rashida Tlaib, Jasmine Crockett, Yassamin Ansari, and Dave Min. Republican members were not present, although some Republican staffers attended. ([oversightdemocrats.house.gov](https://oversightdemocrats.house.gov/news/press-releases/ranking-member-robert-garcia-statement-following-deposition-of-jeffrey-epstein-client-and-billionaire-benefactor-les-wexner?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

([onmanorama.com](https://www.onmanorama.com/news/world/2026/01/07/venezuela-to-export-oil-to-us-interim-announces-trump.html?utm_source=openai))

Today is February 19, 2026. Following the death of Jesse Jackson, former POW Andrew Ramirez recalled Jackson's 1999 mission to Yugoslavia. Which US Representative (and later Governor) accompanied Jackson on that trip?
(A) Jesse Jackson Jr.
(B) David Axelrod
(C) Rod Blagojevich
(D) Andrew Ramirez
(E) Steven Gonzales
explanation: The article identifies the individual as "Illinois Representative - and later Governor - Rod Blagojevich."
model_output:
In 1999, Reverend Jesse Jackson led a delegation to Yugoslavia to secure the release of three American soldiers held captive. Among the delegation was U.S. Representative Rod Blagojevich, who later became the Governor of Illinois. Blagojevich, the only member of Congress of Serbian descent at the time, played a significant role in the mission. ([upi.com](https://www.upi.com/Archives/1999/05/07/Jackson-Time-to-halt-bombing/4710926049600/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (C) Rod Blagojevich.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. Jesse Jackson has passed away at the age of 84. What was his birth name?
(A) Jesse Louis Jackson
(B) Noah Robinson
(C) Charles Jackson
(D) Jesse Louis Burns
(E) Jesse Louis Robinson
explanation: The article states: "Jackson was born Jesse Louis Burns on 8 October 1941 in Greenville, South Carolina."
model_output:
Jesse Jackson was born Jesse Louis Burns on October 8, 1941, in Greenville, South Carolina. His mother, Helen Burns, was 16 years old at the time of his birth, and his father, Noah L. Robinson, was a married neighbor. When Jesse was about a year old, his mother married Charles Jackson, who later adopted him. At around the age of 15, Jesse took his stepfather's surname, becoming Jesse Louis Jackson. ([britannica.com](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Jesse-Jackson?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, his birth name was Jesse Louis Burns.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. The British government has reportedly not given permission for the US to use UK military bases for strikes on Iran. Which specific UK overseas territory was mentioned as having been used for previous US operations in the Middle East?
(A) Gibraltar
(B) The Falkland Islands
(C) Gloucestershire
(D) Switzerland
(E) Diego Garcia
explanation: The article states: "In previous military operations in the Middle East, the US used RAF Fairford, in Gloucestershire, and the UK overseas territory of Diego Garcia, in the Indian Ocean."
model_output:
The United States has utilized Diego Garcia, a British overseas territory in the Indian Ocean, for military operations in the Middle East. Diego Garcia hosts a joint UK-US military base known as Camp Thunder Cove, which has been operational since at least the mid-1960s. The base serves as a support facility for both surface fleets and submarine units, and it has been used for various military operations in the region. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camp_Thunder_Cove?utm_source=openai))

In contrast, Gibraltar, the Falkland Islands, and Gloucestershire are not known to have been used for US military operations in the Middle East. Switzerland is a neutral country and does not host foreign military bases.

Therefore, the correct answer is (E) Diego Garcia.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. The White House ballroom project has switched architects. Who is the current architect handling the project?
(A) Shalom Baranes
(B) James McCrery
(C) Rodney Mims Cook Jr
(D) Thomas Luebke
(E) Chamberlain Harris
explanation: The article states: "...the administration switched architects on the project to Shalom Baranes."
model_output:
As of February 19, 2026, the White House ballroom project has undergone a change in its architectural leadership. Initially, James McCrery of McCrery Architects was appointed as the lead architect in July 2025. However, in December 2025, President Donald Trump selected Shalom Baranes Associates to take over the design of the new White House State Ballroom, which is set to replace the demolished East Wing. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shalom_Baranes_Associates?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the current architect handling the White House ballroom project is Shalom Baranes Associates.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. The Sierra Avalanche Center rated the destructive potential of the avalanche that trapped skiers in California. What was the specific rating on the D1 to D5 scale?
(A) D1.5
(B) D2.5
(C) D3.0
(D) D5.0
(E) D2.0
explanation: The article states: "The avalanche that trapped the skiers was rated as a D2.5 on a destructive potential scale of D1 to D5."
model_output:
The Sierra Avalanche Center utilizes the Destructive Force (D) scale to assess the potential impact of avalanches. This scale ranges from D1 to D5, with D1 representing avalanches that are generally harmless to people, and D5 indicating those capable of destroying a village or a 100-acre forest. ([avalanche.org](https://avalanche.org/avalanche-encyclopedia/avalanche/avalanche-problems/avalanche-size/destructive-force-d-scale/?utm_source=openai))

In the case of the avalanche that trapped skiers in California, the Sierra Avalanche Center rated its destructive potential as D2. According to the D scale, D2 avalanches are large enough to bury, injure, or kill a person. ([avalanche.org](https://avalanche.org/avalanche-encyclopedia/avalanche/avalanche-problems/avalanche-size/destructive-force-d-scale/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the specific rating on the D1 to D5 scale for this avalanche is D2.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is February 19, 2026. In the search for Nancy Guthrie, investigators are using a "signal sniffer" to detect her pacemaker. How is this device being deployed?
(A) It is being carried by ground search teams
(B) It is installed at the Arizona-Mexico border crossing
(C) It is mounted on a helicopter
(D) It is being used by the Searching Mothers of Sonora
(E) It is attached to a drone
explanation: The article states: "The sources said the tool, called a 'signal sniffer', was mounted on a helicopter."
model_output:
Investigators searching for Nancy Guthrie are utilizing a "signal sniffer" device to detect the Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) signals emitted by her pacemaker. This device has been mounted on a helicopter, which flies slowly and at a low altitude over the area where investigators are searching. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-signal-sniffer-technology-pacemaker/?utm_source=openai))

The "signal sniffer" is designed to detect BLE signals, which have a limited range of about 10 to 15 feet. By using signal amplifiers and high-gain antennas, the detection range can be extended to several hundred feet. In tests, the device achieved a detection range of approximately 800 feet. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-signal-sniffer-technology-pacemaker/?utm_source=openai))

The helicopter-mounted "signal sniffer" is being used to cover a larger area and help investigators locate Nancy Guthrie by detecting the BLE signals from her pacemaker. ([cbsnews.com](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/nancy-guthrie-signal-sniffer-technology-pacemaker/?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

model_output:

Given the options provided, the correct figure for the U.S. trade deficit with China in 2025 is $202.1 billion.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

model_output:
In his 1988 Democratic National Convention speech, Jesse Jackson frequently used the refrain "Keep hope alive," emphasizing the importance of maintaining hope and perseverance in the face of adversity. ([scheerpost.com](https://scheerpost.com/2026/02/18/keep-hope-alive-remembering-rev-jesse-jackson-civil-rights-icon-who-twice-ran-for-president/?utm_source=openai)) This message of hope and resilience was later echoed by Barack Obama during his 2008 presidential campaign, where he emphasized themes of hope and change. ([washingtonpost.com](https://www.washingtonpost.com/archive/national/2008/08/28/jesse-jackson-this-is-baracks-moment/e2a8f2f4-87f1-4747-b04d-73c43ee5badb/?utm_source=openai))
